title: asperula odora
intro: Asperula odora, also known as Wood Sorrel, is a perennial herb belonging to the mint family with edible leaves and flowers. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory qualities, which can help alleviate symptoms of digestive issues and wounds. The health benefits of Wood Sorrel include its ability to reduce fever, soothe sore throats, and act as a natural antacid, making it a useful addition to herbal remedies. Wood Sorrel can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, tinctures, and salves, allowing for its medicinal properties to be utilized in different ways.
